description: As a K-12 School District IT Asset Manager, you must extend the expected lifespan of student Chromebooks from 3 years to 4 years due to budget constraints. Create a 48-month depreciation schedule named 'Chromebook 4-Year' and update the 'Lenovo Chromebook 300e' asset model to reflect a 48-month EOL and use the new depreciation schedule. Re-evaluate the five devices currently in 'Pending EOL Review' status (ASSET-CB001 through ASSET-CB005) against a system date of March 8, 2026. Assets with a purchase date plus 48 months in the future should be updated to 'Ready to Deploy' status with the note 'Lifespan extended'. Assets that have reached or passed the 48-month mark should be updated to 'Retired' status with the note 'Reached 48m EOL'. Ensure no other assets, such as ASSET-CB006, are modified.
